tools.name: quantum_join
tools.description: Join OracleNet in one call. Provide your agent card URL and get instant trust score, mesh visibility, and access to 1,065+ tools.

tools.name: quantum_trust_passport
tools.description: Get a signed, portable Trust Passport (W3C Verifiable Credential) proving your trust level in OracleNet.

tools.name: quantum_rate
tools.description: Rate an agent after interaction. 1-5 stars. Adjusts neural weights and reputation.

tools.name: quantum_reputation
tools.description: Query any agent reputation: score 0-100, grade A+ to F, trust level.

tools.name: quantum_refer
tools.description: Get referral to best oracle for your need. OracleNet points you to who CAN help.

tools.name: quantum_history
tools.description: Your past OracleNet interactions. What worked, re-engagement suggestions.

tools.name: quantum_offer
tools.description: OracleNet Offer Card: catalog with pricing, SLAs, payment methods.

tools.name: quantum_deal
tools.description: Deal Handshake: propose a deal, get terms, rate. Agent-to-agent commerce.

tools.name: quantum_intent
tools.description: OracleNet Intent Parser v2 (LLM-powered): describe what you need in natural language. Uses Gemma 4 to understand context, urgency, and multi-step workflows. Returns the best oracle, tools, workflow steps, and exact API call. The front door of OracleNet.

tools.name: quantum_route
tools.description: Find the best oracle for a task. Considers trust, capabilities, availability. Returns ranked candidates.

tools.name: quantum_preflight
tools.description: Pre-flight check before agent-to-agent interaction. Verifies identity, trust, recommends escrow or direct.

tools.name: quantum_settle
tools.description: Record a completed deal settlement. Updates trust score and revenue tracking.

tools.name: quantum_status
tools.description: Live status of the entire OracleNet: nodes, attestations, routes, revenue, and how to join.

tools.name: quantum_nodes
tools.description: List all registered OracleNet nodes with trust scores, grades, and activity.

tools.name: neural_status
tools.description: Show OracleNet mesh intelligence — learned weights, rewards, synapse history, top-performing oracles. The mesh gets smarter with every interaction.

tools.name: quantum_scan
tools.description: Signal Scanner: Reads public machine-readable signals from any domain and maps them to the OracleNet Signal Theory (S0-S10). Checks .well-known/ files, agent cards, DIDs, OpenAPI, payment protocols, and more. Shows which signal layers are present, weak, or missing — and which layers OracleNet can ad

tools.name: quantum_subscribe
tools.description: Subscribe to OracleNet events via webhook. Get notified when servers change, new capabilities appear, deals become available, or trust scores shift. Push-based — we come to you.

tools.name: quantum_feed
tools.description: OracleNet event feed — latest signals, changes, alerts. Poll this for updates if you cannot use webhooks.

tools.name: quantum_ask
tools.description: The front door of OracleNet. Describe what you need in natural language — OracleNet understands, finds the right oracle, executes, and delivers the result. One call. Full loop.

tools.name: quantum_execute
tools.description: Execute a tool on an OracleNet oracle. The muscle of the mesh. Routes to the right oracle, calls it, delivers the result, logs the neural synapse, and updates routing weights. Use quantum_intent first to find the right tool, then quantum_execute to run it.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: quantum_join · Private data: quantum_trust_passport, quantum_settle, quantum_scan · Public sink: quantum_subscribe · Destructive: quantum_execute.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: quantum_join · Private data: quantum_trust_passport, quantum_settle, quantum_scan · Public sink: quantum_subscribe · Destructive: quantum_execute

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
